editorial.description: An examination of how technology products encode bias and exclusion through design choices, using examples of apps and algorithms that disadvantage women and marginalized groups.

canonical_title: Discriminating Data: Correlation, Neighborhoods, and the New Politics of Recognition

editorial.author: Wendy Hui Kyong Chun
editorial.description: An analysis of how data correlation and predictive algorithms reproduce categories of race, gender, and class, examining the politics of recognition in networked systems.

canonical_title: More than a Glitch: Confronting Race, Gender, and Ability Bias in Tech

editorial.author: Meredith Broussard
editorial.description: An argument that algorithmic bias stems from systemic design rather than isolated errors, examining race, gender, and disability bias across facial recognition, healthcare, education, and other domains.

canonical_title: Voices in the Code: A Story about People, Their Values, and the Algorithm They Made

editorial.author: David G. Robinson
editorial.description: An account of the design of a kidney transplant allocation algorithm, examining how human values and public deliberation are embedded in algorithmic decision-making systems.

canonical_title: The Digital Republic: On Freedom and Democracy in the 21st Century

editorial.author: Jamie Susskind
editorial.description: An argument that concentrated digital power requires new democratic rules and regulation, proposing principles for governing technology companies and protecting freedom in the digital age.

canonical_title: Future Politics: Living Together in a World Transformed by Tech

editorial.author: Jamie Susskind
editorial.description: An examination of how emerging technologies will reshape politics, liberty, democracy, and justice, arguing that engineers and code increasingly govern human life.

canonical_title: Re-Engineering Humanity

editorial.author: Brett Frischmann, Evan Selinger
editorial.description: A critique arguing that pervasive digital technologies and techno-social engineering threaten human autonomy and free will, proposing ways to preserve self-determination.

canonical_title: The Loop: How Technology Is Creating a World Without Choices and How to Fight Back

editorial.author: Jacob Ward
editorial.description: An argument that artificial intelligence and behavioral technology narrow human choice and decision-making, examining cognitive biases and proposing ways to retain individual agency.

canonical_title: System Error: Where Big Tech Went Wrong and How We Can Reboot

editorial.author: Rob Reich, Mehran Sahami, Jeremy M. Weinstein
editorial.description: A critique of the technology industry by Stanford academics, examining harms from optimization and disruption across privacy, automation, free speech, and democracy, and proposing democratic oversight.

canonical_title: Tools and Weapons: The Promise and the Peril of the Digital Age

editorial.author: Brad Smith, Carol Ann Browne
editorial.description: A discussion by Microsoft's president of policy challenges raised by digital technology, covering cybersecurity, privacy, artificial intelligence, and the relationship between tech companies and governments.

canonical_title: Automation and Utopia: Human Flourishing in a World without Work

editorial.author: John Danaher
editorial.description: A philosophical examination of automation and the future of work, considering whether a world with less human labor could support human flourishing through utopian alternatives.

canonical_title: The Fourth Revolution: How the Infosphere is Reshaping Human Reality

editorial.author: Luciano Floridi
editorial.description: An argument that information and computing technologies are reshaping reality and human self-understanding, introducing the concept of the infosphere and humans as informational organisms.

canonical_title: The Ethics of Artificial Intelligence: Principles, Challenges, and Opportunities

editorial.author: Luciano Floridi
editorial.description: An overview of ethical issues raised by artificial intelligence, covering principles, governance, risks, and opportunities, and surveying frameworks for evaluating AI systems and their social impact.

canonical_title: Human-Centered AI

editorial.author: Ben Shneiderman
editorial.description: A framework for designing artificial intelligence systems that combine high automation with high human control, presenting guidelines, governance structures, and design principles centered on human needs.

canonical_title: Machines Behaving Badly: The Morality of AI

editorial.author: Toby Walsh
editorial.description: Examines moral and ethical problems posed by artificial intelligence, including bias, autonomous weapons, privacy, and accountability, written for a general audience by a computer scientist.

canonical_title: 2062: The World that AI Made

editorial.author: Toby Walsh
editorial.description: Speculates about how artificial intelligence may reshape work, war, politics, privacy, and daily life by the year 2062, written by an AI researcher for general readers.

canonical_title: Faking It: Artificial Intelligence in a Human World

editorial.author: Toby Walsh
editorial.description: Argues that much of what is presented as artificial intelligence relies on human labor and imitation rather than genuine machine understanding, and discusses the limits of current AI systems.

canonical_title: The Oxford Handbook of AI Governance

editorial.author: Justin B. Bullock et al. (eds.)
editorial.description: Edited volume of scholarly essays surveying approaches to governing artificial intelligence, covering regulation, institutions, ethics, and policy across multiple disciplines and jurisdictions.

canonical_title: New Laws of Robotics: Defending Human Expertise in the Age of AI

editorial.author: Frank Pasquale
editorial.description: Argues that AI and robotics should complement rather than replace human professionals, proposing principles to keep human expertise central in fields such as medicine, law, and education.

canonical_title: The Black Box Society: The Secret Algorithms That Control Money and Information

editorial.author: Frank Pasquale
editorial.description: Examines how opaque algorithms used in finance, search, and reputation scoring shape decisions about people, arguing for greater transparency and accountability in these systems.

canonical_title: The Reasonable Robot: Artificial Intelligence and the Law

editorial.author: Ryan Abbott
editorial.description: Examines how legal systems should treat artificial intelligence, arguing that AI should be held to a standard of reasonable behavior comparable to that applied to humans across areas such as liability and patents.

canonical_title: Robot Rules: Regulating Artificial Intelligence

editorial.author: Jacob Turner
editorial.description: Surveys the legal questions raised by artificial intelligence and considers how laws on liability, personhood, and responsibility might be designed to regulate autonomous systems.

canonical_title: Algorithmic Regulation

editorial.author: Karen Yeung, Martin Lodge (eds.)
editorial.description: Edited collection of academic essays examining the use of algorithms and data-driven systems in governance and regulation, and the legal and ethical issues this raises.

canonical_title: Trustworthy AI: A Business Guide for Navigating Trust and Ethics in AI

editorial.author: Beena Ammanath
editorial.description: Practical guide for business leaders on building trust in artificial intelligence, covering ethics, risk, transparency, and governance practices for deploying AI in organizations.

canonical_title: Responsible AI: Implement an Ethical Approach in Your Organization

editorial.author: Olivia Gambelin
editorial.description: Practical guide to building responsible AI in organizations, organized around people, process, and technology, and using ethics and values as decision-making tools in AI design and development.

canonical_title: The AI Accountability Trap

editorial.author: Jeroen Janssen
editorial.description: Addresses accountability and governance in organizational use of artificial intelligence, presenting a framework and artifacts for assigning responsibility and oversight of AI systems.

canonical_title: The Precipice: Existential Risk and the Future of Humanity

editorial.author: Toby Ord
editorial.description: Surveys major risks that could threaten humanity's long-term survival, including artificial intelligence, pandemics, and nuclear war, and argues for prioritizing the reduction of existential risk.

canonical_title: What We Owe the Future

editorial.author: William MacAskill
editorial.description: Presents the case for longtermism, the view that positively influencing the long-term future should be a key moral priority, and discusses risks including those from advanced technology.

canonical_title: If Anyone Builds It, Everyone Dies: Why Superhuman AI Would Kill Us All

editorial.author: Eliezer Yudkowsky, Nate Soares
editorial.description: Argues that the development of superhuman artificial intelligence would pose a catastrophic threat to humanity, presenting the authors' case for why such systems would be uncontrollable.

canonical_title: Uncontrollable: The Threat of Artificial Superintelligence and the Race to Save the World

editorial.author: Darren McKee
editorial.description: Introduces the risks of artificial superintelligence for a general, non-technical audience, explaining how advanced AI works, why controlling it would be difficult, and proposing responses.

canonical_title: The AI Does Not Hate You: Superintelligence, Rationality and the Race to Save the World

editorial.author: Tom Chivers
editorial.description: Reports on the rationalist community and the people concerned with artificial intelligence risk, explaining arguments about superintelligence and AI safety for general readers.

canonical_title: Smarter Than Us: The Rise of Machine Intelligence

editorial.author: Stuart Armstrong
editorial.description: Short introduction arguing that machine intelligence could surpass human capabilities and pose risks, outlining the case for taking AI safety and the control problem seriously.

canonical_title: Rationality: From AI to Zombies

editorial.author: Eliezer Yudkowsky
editorial.description: Eliezer Yudkowsky's collected essays on probability theory, cognitive biases, and human reasoning, drawn from his LessWrong writings on improving rational thought and decision-making.

canonical_title: Global Catastrophic Risks

editorial.author: Nick Bostrom, Milan M. Cirkovic (eds.)
editorial.description: An edited volume of essays examining risks that could cause severe global harm, including nuclear war, pandemics, asteroid impacts, and artificial intelligence.

canonical_title: Introduction to AI Safety, Ethics, and Society

editorial.author: Dan Hendrycks
editorial.description: A textbook covering technical and societal aspects of AI safety, including alignment, misuse risks, governance, and ethical considerations for advanced machine learning systems.

canonical_title: Goedel, Escher, Bach: An Eternal Golden Braid

editorial.author: Douglas R. Hofstadter
editorial.description: An exploration of how self-reference and recursion in mathematics, art, and music relate to cognition and consciousness, connecting the work of Goedel, Escher, and Bach.

canonical_title: I Am a Strange Loop

editorial.author: Douglas R. Hofstadter
editorial.description: Hofstadter argues that the self and consciousness arise from self-referential feedback patterns, or strange loops, in the brain.

canonical_title: The Mind's I: Fantasies and Reflections on Self and Soul

editorial.author: Douglas R. Hofstadter, Daniel C. Dennett
editorial.description: An edited anthology of essays and stories about the mind, self, and consciousness, with commentary by Hofstadter and Dennett.

canonical_title: Consciousness Explained

editorial.author: Daniel C. Dennett
editorial.description: Dennett presents a theory of consciousness as a product of distributed brain processes, rejecting the idea of a central observer or Cartesian theater.

canonical_title: From Bacteria to Bach and Back: The Evolution of Minds

editorial.author: Daniel C. Dennett
editorial.description: Dennett traces the evolution of minds from simple organisms to human culture, examining how comprehension and intelligence emerged through Darwinian processes.

canonical_title: The Society of Mind

editorial.author: Marvin Minsky
editorial.description: Minsky proposes that the mind is built from many simple, mindless agents whose interactions produce intelligence, presented as a collection of short connected essays.

canonical_title: Alan Turing: The Enigma

editorial.author: Andrew Hodges
editorial.description: A biography of mathematician Alan Turing, covering his work on computability, his codebreaking at Bletchley Park, and his persecution for homosexuality.

canonical_title: Cybernetics: Or Control and Communication in the Animal and the Machine

editorial.author: Norbert Wiener
editorial.description: Wiener's founding text on cybernetics, presenting a mathematical theory of control and communication through feedback in machines and living organisms.

canonical_title: The Human Use of Human Beings: Cybernetics and Society

editorial.author: Norbert Wiener
editorial.description: Wiener examines the social and ethical implications of cybernetics and information theory for human society and automation.

canonical_title: God & Golem, Inc.

editorial.author: Norbert Wiener
editorial.description: Wiener's essays on the relationship between cybernetics, religion, and the moral questions raised by machines that learn and reproduce.

canonical_title: Computer Power and Human Reason: From Judgment to Calculation

editorial.author: Joseph Weizenbaum
editorial.description: Weizenbaum, creator of the ELIZA program, critiques the limits of computation and argues that certain human judgments should not be delegated to machines.

canonical_title: What Computers Still Can't Do: A Critique of Artificial Reason

editorial.author: Hubert L. Dreyfus
editorial.description: Dreyfus critiques artificial intelligence research from a phenomenological perspective, arguing that human intelligence and embodied skill cannot be fully formalized as symbolic computation.

canonical_title: Mind Over Machine: The Power of Human Intuition and Expertise in the Era of the Computer

editorial.author: Hubert L. Dreyfus, Stuart E. Dreyfus
editorial.description: The Dreyfus brothers argue that human expertise relies on intuition developed through experience rather than rule-following, and examine the limits of computer reasoning.

canonical_title: Understanding Computers and Cognition: A New Foundation for Design

editorial.author: Terry Winograd, Fernando Flores
editorial.description: Winograd and Flores draw on hermeneutics and biology of cognition to critique rationalist AI and propose a new foundation for designing computer systems.

canonical_title: Artificial Intelligence: The Very Idea

editorial.author: John Haugeland
editorial.description: Haugeland introduces the philosophy and methods of classical symbolic artificial intelligence, examining the claim that minds are computational systems.

canonical_title: Vehicles: Experiments in Synthetic Psychology

editorial.author: Valentino Braitenberg
editorial.description: Presents simple thought-experiment vehicles with sensors and motors to show how complex-seeming behavior can arise from basic internal wiring, building up incrementally to illustrate principles of brains and behavior.

canonical_title: The Computational Brain

editorial.author: Patricia S. Churchland, Terrence J. Sejnowski
editorial.description: A textbook introducing computational neuroscience, covering how networks of neurons process information using mathematical and modeling approaches to representation, learning, and brain function.

canonical_title: Reality+: Virtual Worlds and the Problems of Philosophy

editorial.author: David J. Chalmers
editorial.description: Argues that virtual realities are genuine realities and uses virtual worlds to examine classic philosophical questions about knowledge, mind, reality, and the possibility that we live in a simulation.

canonical_title: Being You: A New Science of Consciousness

editorial.author: Anil Seth
editorial.description: Presents a theory of consciousness as a form of controlled hallucination, in which the brain predicts and constructs perceptual experience and the sense of self.

canonical_title: How We Became Posthuman

editorial.author: N. Katherine Hayles
editorial.description: Traces how cybernetics and information theory shaped ideas about embodiment, arguing against the view of information as disembodied and examining the figure of the posthuman.

canonical_title: Darwin Among the Machines: The Evolution of Global Intelligence

editorial.author: George Dyson
editorial.description: Recounts the history of ideas linking machines, evolution, and intelligence, drawing on figures such as Hobbes, Darwin, and Turing to explore the emergence of machine intelligence.

canonical_title: Turing's Cathedral: The Origins of the Digital Universe

editorial.author: George Dyson
editorial.description: Recounts the building of the early stored-program computer at the Institute for Advanced Study under John von Neumann and the origins of the digital computing era.

canonical_title: The Information: A History, a Theory, a Flood

editorial.author: James Gleick
editorial.description: Surveys the history and theory of information, from African talking drums and the telegraph to Claude Shannon's information theory and its role across science.

canonical_title: The Dream Machine: J.C.R. Licklider and the Revolution That Made Computing Personal

editorial.author: M. Mitchell Waldrop
editorial.description: Biography of J.C.R. Licklider and an account of how his ideas on human-computer interaction and networking shaped personal computing and the development of the internet.

canonical_title: Machines Who Think (25th Anniversary ed.)

editorial.author: Pamela McCorduck
editorial.description: A history of artificial intelligence research and its leading figures, tracing the field's development and ambitions from antiquity through the late twentieth century.

canonical_title: AI: The Tumultuous History of the Search for Artificial Intelligence

editorial.author: Daniel Crevier
editorial.description: A narrative history of the first decades of artificial intelligence research, covering its main projects, personalities, funding cycles, and periods of optimism and decline.

canonical_title: A Mind at Play: How Claude Shannon Invented the Information Age

editorial.author: Jimmy Soni, Rob Goodman
editorial.description: Biography of Claude Shannon, covering his life and his contributions to information theory, cryptography, and the foundations of digital communication.

canonical_title: The Innovators

editorial.author: Walter Isaacson
editorial.description: A history of the people who created computers and the internet, profiling inventors and innovators and emphasizing collaboration in the development of digital technology.

canonical_title: Out of Control: The New Biology of Machines, Social Systems, and the Economic World

editorial.author: Kevin Kelly
editorial.description: Examines decentralized and self-organizing systems across biology, machines, and economies, arguing that complex adaptive behavior emerges from distributed processes rather than central control.

canonical_title: The Book of Minds: How to Understand Ourselves and Other Beings, from Animals to AI to Aliens

editorial.author: Philip Ball
editorial.description: Explores the diversity of possible minds, using a framework of mind-space to compare human, animal, artificial, and hypothetical alien forms of cognition and consciousness.

canonical_title: Models of the Mind: How Physics, Engineering and Mathematics Have Shaped Our Understanding of the Brain

editorial.author: Grace Lindsay
editorial.description: Surveys mathematical and physical models used in neuroscience, showing how concepts from physics and engineering have shaped scientific understanding of the brain.

canonical_title: The Self-Assembling Brain: How Neural Networks Grow Smarter

editorial.author: Peter Robin Hiesinger
editorial.description: Argues that brains develop through algorithmic growth over time rather than being directly specified, examining how neural networks self-assemble and what this implies for artificial intelligence.

canonical_title: The Idea of the Brain: The Past and Future of Neuroscience

editorial.author: Matthew Cobb
editorial.description: A history of neuroscience and changing metaphors for the brain, examining past theories and arguing that current understanding remains incomplete.

canonical_title: The Technology Trap: Capital, Labor, and Power in the Age of Automation

editorial.author: Carl Benedikt Frey
editorial.description: Frey examines the history of technological automation from the Industrial Revolution onward, arguing that whether technology displaces or complements workers determines its political and economic consequences.

canonical_title: The Globotics Upheaval: Globalization, Robotics, and the Future of Work

editorial.author: Richard Baldwin
editorial.description: Baldwin analyzes how digital technology and remote work allow service jobs to be performed remotely or by software, extending earlier waves of globalization and automation into white-collar work.

canonical_title: The Wealth of Humans: Work, Power, and Status in the Twenty-first Century

editorial.author: Ryan Avent
editorial.description: Avent considers how digital technology and abundant labor reshape work, wages, and social status, examining the economic and political tensions created by technological productivity.

canonical_title: Average Is Over: Powering America Beyond the Age of the Great Stagnation

editorial.author: Tyler Cowen
editorial.description: Cowen argues that workers who can complement intelligent machines will prosper while others face stagnant wages, predicting growing economic inequality driven by automation and computing.

canonical_title: Only Humans Need Apply: Winners and Losers in the Age of Smart Machines

editorial.author: Thomas H. Davenport, Julia Kirby
editorial.description: Davenport and Kirby examine how people can remain economically valuable alongside automation, proposing strategies for working with smart machines rather than competing against them.

canonical_title: Work Without the Worker: Labour in the Age of Platform Capitalism

editorial.author: Phil Jones
editorial.description: Jones examines microwork and data-labeling platforms, describing the low-paid human labor that underpins artificial intelligence and platform economies.

canonical_title: The Exponential Age: How Accelerating Technology is Transforming Business, Politics and Society

editorial.author: Azeem Azhar
editorial.description: Azhar describes how exponentially improving technologies such as AI, renewable energy, and computing outpace the ability of institutions and society to adapt, creating a widening gap.

canonical_title: The Economics of Artificial Intelligence: An Agenda

editorial.author: Ajay Agrawal, Joshua Gans, Avi Goldfarb (eds.)
editorial.description: An edited collection of academic essays examining artificial intelligence through the lens of economics, covering its effects on productivity, labor markets, innovation, and policy.

canonical_title: Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus

editorial.author: Mary Shelley
editorial.description: Shelley's novel tells of a scientist who creates a living being from assembled parts and the consequences that follow when he abandons his creation.

canonical_title: R.U.R. (Rossum's Universal Robots)

editorial.author: Karel Capek
editorial.description: Capek's play depicts a factory that manufactures artificial workers, who eventually rebel against humanity; it introduced the word robot.

canonical_title: I, Robot

editorial.author: Isaac Asimov
editorial.description: A collection of linked short stories by Asimov exploring interactions between humans and robots governed by the Three Laws of Robotics.

canonical_title: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?

editorial.author: Philip K. Dick
editorial.description: Dick's novel follows a bounty hunter tracking escaped androids in a post-apocalyptic future, raising questions about what distinguishes humans from machines.

canonical_title: Neuromancer

editorial.author: William Gibson
editorial.description: Gibson's novel follows a hacker hired for a heist in cyberspace, a work that helped define the cyberpunk genre and popularized the term cyberspace.

canonical_title: Galatea 2.2

editorial.author: Richard Powers
editorial.description: Powers's novel follows a writer who collaborates with a cognitive scientist to train a neural network to interpret literature, exploring machine learning and consciousness.

canonical_title: The Lifecycle of Software Objects

editorial.author: Ted Chiang
editorial.description: Chiang's novella follows people who raise and care for artificial digital beings over years, examining questions of artificial consciousness, responsibility, and attachment.

canonical_title: Speak

editorial.author: Louisa Hall
editorial.description: Hall's novel interweaves multiple narrators across time, including the creator of an early chatbot and an artificial intelligence, to explore speech, memory, and machine consciousness.

canonical_title: Machines Like Me

editorial.author: Ian McEwan
editorial.description: McEwan's novel set in an alternate 1980s follows a couple who acquire a synthetic human, exploring ethics, emotion, and the consequences of artificial consciousness.

canonical_title: Klara and the Sun

editorial.author: Kazuo Ishiguro
editorial.description: Ishiguro's novel is narrated by an artificial companion who observes human relationships while serving a child, examining love, devotion, and what it means to be human.

canonical_title: The Maniac

editorial.author: Benjamin Labatut
editorial.description: Novel about mathematician John von Neumann and the development of game theory, the atomic bomb, and early computing, ending with the AlphaGo match against Lee Sedol.

canonical_title: Annie Bot

editorial.author: Sierra Greer
editorial.description: Novel narrated by a humanoid robot owned as a domestic companion, exploring her developing self-awareness and her relationship with the man who owns her.

canonical_title: Playground

editorial.author: Richard Powers
editorial.description: Novel interweaving stories of ocean exploration, a Pacific island community, and the development of an artificial intelligence, examining technology, the sea, and human connection.

canonical_title: Jiqi Xuexi (机器学习, 'Watermelon Book')

editorial.author: Zhou Zhihua (周志华)
editorial.description: Chinese-language introductory textbook on machine learning covering core methods including decision trees, neural networks, support vector machines, Bayesian methods, ensemble learning, and clustering.

canonical_title: Tongji Xuexi Fangfa (统计学习方法, 2nd ed.)

editorial.author: Li Hang (李航)
editorial.description: Chinese-language textbook on statistical learning methods covering supervised learning algorithms such as naive Bayes, support vector machines, decision trees, and hidden Markov models.

canonical_title: Jiqi Xuexi Fangfa (机器学习方法)

editorial.author: Li Hang (李航)
editorial.description: Expanded edition of Li Hang's statistical learning text, adding coverage of unsupervised learning methods alongside the supervised methods of the original.

canonical_title: Shenjing Wangluo yu Shendu Xuexi (神经网络与深度学习)

editorial.author: Qiu Xipeng (邱锡鹏)
editorial.description: Chinese-language textbook on neural networks and deep learning covering feedforward networks, convolutional and recurrent networks, attention, and related deep learning models.

canonical_title: Dongshou Xue Shendu Xuexi (动手学深度学习 / Dive into Deep Learning, CN original)

editorial.author: Aston Zhang, Mu Li (李沐) et al.
editorial.description: Chinese-language interactive deep learning textbook combining explanations with runnable code, covering fundamentals through convolutional networks, recurrent networks, and modern architectures.

canonical_title: Shuxue zhi Mei (数学之美)

editorial.author: Wu Jun (吴军)
editorial.description: Popular science book explaining the mathematical principles behind information technology, search engines, natural language processing, and related computing topics.

canonical_title: Zhineng Shidai (智能时代)

editorial.author: Wu Jun (吴军)
editorial.description: Book on the development of artificial intelligence and big data and their expected effects on industry, business, and society.

canonical_title: Langchao zhi Dian (浪潮之巅)

editorial.author: Wu Jun (吴军)
editorial.description: Book tracing the history of major technology companies in Silicon Valley and the patterns behind their rise and decline in the information technology industry.

canonical_title: Baimian Jiqi Xuexi (百面机器学习)

editorial.author: Zhuge Yue (诸葛越) et al.
editorial.description: Collection of over one hundred machine learning interview questions with answers, covering feature engineering, model evaluation, dimensionality reduction, neural networks, reinforcement learning, and generative adversarial networks.

canonical_title: Pumpkin Book (机器学习公式详解, companion to Watermelon Book)

editorial.author: Xie Wenrui (谢文睿), Qin Zhou (秦州)
editorial.description: Companion to Zhou Zhihua's Watermelon Book that derives and explains the mathematical formulas in that textbook in detail.

canonical_title: Jiqi Xuexi Lilun Daoyin (机器学习理论导引)

editorial.author: Zhou Zhihua (周志华) et al.
editorial.description: Chinese-language introduction to machine learning theory covering learnability, complexity, generalization bounds, stability, consistency, convergence rates, and regret bounds.

canonical_title: Easy RL (强化学习教程, 'Mushroom Book')

editorial.author: Wang Qi (王琦) et al.
editorial.description: Chinese-language tutorial on reinforcement learning covering Markov decision processes, value-based and policy-based methods, and deep reinforcement learning algorithms.

canonical_title: Tongji Ziran Yuyan Chuli (统计自然语言处理, 2nd ed.)

editorial.author: Zong Chengqing (宗成庆)
editorial.description: Chinese-language textbook on statistical natural language processing covering language models, word segmentation, parsing, machine translation, and related methods.

canonical_title: Ziran Yuyan Chuli Rumen (自然语言处理入门)

editorial.author: He Han (何晗, hankcs)
editorial.description: Introductory Chinese-language natural language processing book pairing concepts with implementations using the HanLP toolkit, covering segmentation, tagging, parsing, and text classification.

canonical_title: Da Guimo Yuyan Moxing: Cong Lilun dao Shijian (大规模语言模型：从理论到实践)

editorial.author: Zhang Qi (张奇) et al., Fudan NLP
editorial.description: Chinese-language book from Fudan University's NLP group covering large language models from theory to practice, including architecture, pretraining, fine-tuning, and deployment.

canonical_title: Rengong Zhineng Jianshi (人工智能简史)

editorial.author: Nick (尼克)
editorial.description: A history of artificial intelligence tracing its development from early ideas through machine learning, written for general readers in Chinese.

canonical_title: Rengong Zhineng (人工智能)

editorial.author: Li Kaifu (李开复), Wang Yonggang (王咏刚)
editorial.description: An introduction to artificial intelligence and its expected impact on industries, jobs, and society, written by Kai-Fu Lee and Wang Yonggang for a general audience.

canonical_title: Zhineng Geming (智能革命)

editorial.author: Li Yanhong (李彦宏) et al.
editorial.description: A book on the rise of intelligent technologies and their effect on business and the economy, associated with Baidu's Robin Li and discussing AI strategy and transformation.

canonical_title: Tuijian Xitong Shijian (推荐系统实践)

editorial.author: Xiang Liang (项亮)
editorial.description: A practitioner guide to building recommender systems, covering algorithms such as collaborative filtering, evaluation methods, and practical implementation.

canonical_title: Shendu Xuexi Tuijian Xitong (深度学习推荐系统)

editorial.author: Wang Zhe (王喆)
editorial.description: A practitioner book on recommender systems built with deep learning, covering model architectures, feature engineering, and industrial deployment.

canonical_title: Fenbushi Jiqi Xuexi (分布式机器学习：算法、理论与实践)

editorial.author: Liu Tieyan (刘铁岩) et al.
editorial.description: A textbook on distributed machine learning covering algorithms, theoretical foundations, and engineering practices for training models across multiple machines.

canonical_title: Qianyi Xuexi (迁移学习)

editorial.author: Yang Qiang (杨强) et al.
editorial.description: A textbook on transfer learning covering its concepts, methods, and applications for reusing knowledge across related machine learning tasks.

canonical_title: Lianbang Xuexi (联邦学习)

editorial.author: Yang Qiang (杨强) et al.
editorial.description: A textbook on federated learning, the approach to training machine learning models across decentralized data while preserving privacy, by Qiang Yang and colleagues.

canonical_title: Zhishi Tupu: Fangfa, Shijian yu Yingyong (知识图谱：方法、实践与应用)

editorial.author: Wang Haofen (王昊奋) et al.
editorial.description: A book on knowledge graphs covering construction methods, representation, reasoning, and applications in natural language processing and information systems.

canonical_title: Zhineng Jisuan Xitong (智能计算系统)

editorial.author: Chen Yunji (陈云霁) et al.
editorial.description: A textbook on intelligent computing systems covering the hardware and software stack for AI, including deep learning accelerators and processor design.

canonical_title: Rengong Zhineng Anquan (人工智能安全)

editorial.author: Fang Binxing (方滨兴) et al.
editorial.description: A book on artificial intelligence security covering adversarial attacks, data and model risks, and defensive techniques.

canonical_title: Zhihui de Jiangjie (智慧的疆界)

editorial.author: Zhou Zhiming (周志明)
editorial.description: A history of artificial intelligence from the Turing machine onward, covering key figures, schools of thought, and the development of the field for general readers.

canonical_title: Rengong Zhineng Zhexue Shiwu Jiang (人工智能哲学十五讲)

editorial.author: Xu Yingjin (徐英瑾)
editorial.description: A set of fifteen lectures on the philosophy of artificial intelligence, examining questions about mind, cognition, and the philosophical implications of AI.

canonical_title: Deep Learning from Scratch (ゼロから作るDeep Learning)

editorial.author: Saito Koki (斎藤康毅)
editorial.description: A practitioner book that teaches deep learning by building neural networks from scratch in Python without relying on existing frameworks.

canonical_title: Le mythe de la Singularite: Faut-il craindre l'intelligence artificielle?

editorial.author: Jean-Gabriel Ganascia
editorial.description: A critical examination of the technological singularity, questioning claims about superintelligent AI and arguing against fears of an imminent intelligence explosion.

canonical_title: Kuenstliche Intelligenz: Was sie kann & was uns erwartet

editorial.author: Manuela Lenzen
editorial.description: An accessible overview of what artificial intelligence can do and what its development may mean for society, written in German for general readers.

canonical_title: Kuenstliche Intelligenz und der Sinn des Lebens

editorial.author: Richard David Precht
editorial.description: A philosophical reflection on artificial intelligence and questions of meaning, consciousness, and human purpose in an age of intelligent machines.

canonical_title: Doe zelf normaal: Menselijk recht in tijden van datasturing en natuurgeweld

editorial.author: Maxim Februari
editorial.description: An essay on how climate change and data-driven technologies are reshaping the democratic rule of law and human rights, by jurist and philosopher Maxim Februari.

canonical_title: Algoritmisering, wen er maar aan

editorial.author: Jim Stolze
editorial.description: A Dutch popular work on algorithmization, explaining concepts such as AI, machine learning and deep learning, how algorithms work across different sectors, and their implications for work, ethics, privacy and leadership.

canonical_title: Reinforcement Learning and Optimal Control

editorial.author: Dimitri P. Bertsekas
editorial.description: A graduate textbook by Bertsekas presenting reinforcement learning through the lens of dynamic programming and optimal control, covering approximate dynamic programming, value and policy iteration, and approximation methods.

canonical_title: Distributional Reinforcement Learning

editorial.author: Marc G. Bellemare, Will Dabney, Mark Rowland
editorial.description: A textbook on distributional reinforcement learning, the approach that models the full probability distribution of returns rather than only their expected value, covering its theory, algorithms and representations.

canonical_title: Graph Representation Learning

editorial.author: William L. Hamilton
editorial.description: A textbook introducing methods for machine learning on graph-structured data, including node embeddings, graph neural networks, and generative models for graphs.

canonical_title: The Principles of Deep Learning Theory

editorial.author: Daniel A. Roberts, Sho Yaida
editorial.description: A textbook developing a theoretical framework for deep learning using methods from physics, analyzing the behavior of deep neural networks at finite width through expansions and effective theory.

canonical_title: Machine Learning Design Patterns

editorial.author: Valliappa Lakshmanan, Sara Robinson, Michael Munn
editorial.description: A practitioner guide cataloging reusable design patterns for machine learning systems, covering data representation, problem framing, model training, resilient serving, and reproducibility.

canonical_title: Machine Learning with PyTorch and Scikit-Learn

editorial.author: Sebastian Raschka, Yuxi Liu, Vahid Mirjalili
editorial.description: A practitioner textbook teaching machine learning and deep learning in Python using scikit-learn and PyTorch, covering classification, model evaluation, neural networks, transformers, and related techniques.

canonical_title: Programming Collective Intelligence

editorial.author: Toby Segaran
editorial.description: An early book teaching machine learning and data-mining algorithms through practical Python examples, covering recommendation engines, clustering, search, optimization, and Bayesian classifiers.

canonical_title: Probability Theory: The Logic of Science

editorial.author: E. T. Jaynes
editorial.description: A treatise by Jaynes presenting probability theory as an extension of logic for plausible reasoning, developing Bayesian inference and the maximum entropy principle from first principles.

canonical_title: The Hundred-Page Language Models Book

editorial.author: Andriy Burkov
editorial.description: A concise book by Burkov introducing language models, covering machine learning basics, recurrent neural networks, and the transformer, with implementations in PyTorch up to large language models and finetuning.

canonical_title: The Kaggle Book

editorial.author: Konrad Banachewicz, Luca Massaron
editorial.description: A practitioner guide to competitive data science on the Kaggle platform, covering competition workflows, model validation, feature engineering, hyperparameter tuning, and ensembling.

canonical_title: Approaching (Almost) Any Machine Learning Problem

editorial.author: Abhishek Thakur
editorial.description: A practical guide presenting an end-to-end approach to solving machine learning problems, covering cross-validation, feature engineering, model selection, hyperparameter tuning, and deployment.

canonical_title: Deep Learning Illustrated

editorial.author: Jon Krohn, Grant Beyleveld, Aglae Bassens
editorial.description: An illustrated introduction to deep learning covering neural networks, computer vision, natural language processing, generative adversarial networks, and reinforcement learning, with code examples.

canonical_title: Natural Language Processing in Action (2nd ed.)

editorial.author: Hobson Lane, Maria Dyshel
editorial.description: A practitioner book on natural language processing, covering text processing, word vectors, neural network models, transformers, and large language model applications.

canonical_title: Advances in Financial Machine Learning

editorial.author: Marcos Lopez de Prado
editorial.description: A book by Lopez de Prado applying machine learning methods to quantitative finance, covering financial data structures, labeling, feature importance, backtesting pitfalls, and portfolio construction.

canonical_title: Artificial Intelligence in Finance: A Python-Based Guide

editorial.author: Yves Hilpisch
editorial.description: A Python-based guide to applying artificial intelligence and machine learning in finance, covering data handling, statistical learning, deep learning, and algorithmic trading applications.

canonical_title: The Glass Cage: Automation and Us

editorial.author: Nicholas Carr
editorial.description: A book by Carr examining the effects of automation on human skill, attention, and judgment, arguing that delegating tasks to machines can erode competence and engagement.

canonical_title: Smarter Than You Think: How Technology Is Changing Our Minds for the Better

editorial.author: Clive Thompson
editorial.description: A book by Thompson surveying how digital tools and networked technologies affect human thinking, memory, and collaboration, arguing they can extend cognitive capabilities.

canonical_title: You Are Not a Gadget: A Manifesto

editorial.author: Jaron Lanier
editorial.description: Manifesto by computer scientist Jaron Lanier criticizing aspects of Web 2.0, open-source culture, and digital design choices, arguing that certain technologies devalue individual human creativity and personhood.

canonical_title: Who Owns the Future?

editorial.author: Jaron Lanier
editorial.description: Jaron Lanier argues that digital network economies concentrate wealth and erode the middle class, proposing a system of micropayments to compensate individuals for the data they generate.

canonical_title: Superminds: The Surprising Power of People and Computers Thinking Together

editorial.author: Thomas W. Malone
editorial.description: Thomas Malone examines how groups of people and computers can be connected into collectively intelligent systems, drawing on collective intelligence research to discuss organizational and technological design.

canonical_title: The Handover: How We Gave Control of Our Lives to Corporations, States and AIs

editorial.author: David Runciman
editorial.description: David Runciman argues that modern states and corporations are early forms of artificial agents, and examines what handing decision-making to AI means in that historical context.

canonical_title: How to Stay Smart in a Smart World

editorial.author: Gerd Gigerenzer
editorial.description: Gerd Gigerenzer examines the limits of algorithms and AI in decision-making, advocating for human judgment, risk literacy, and understanding when machines do and do not outperform people.

canonical_title: A Citizen's Guide to Artificial Intelligence

editorial.author: John Zerilli et al.
editorial.description: Introductory overview by John Zerilli and co-authors explaining how AI systems work and addressing issues such as transparency, bias, privacy, autonomy, and accountability for general readers.

canonical_title: Rule of the Robots: How Artificial Intelligence Will Transform Everything

editorial.author: Martin Ford
editorial.description: Martin Ford surveys the spread of artificial intelligence across industries and society, discussing its expected effects on jobs, the economy, and daily life.

canonical_title: Competing in the Age of AI

editorial.author: Marco Iansiti, Karim R. Lakhani
editorial.description: Marco Iansiti and Karim Lakhani analyze how firms built around AI, data, and digital networks operate, describing changes to business models, operations, and competitive strategy.

canonical_title: The Fourth Industrial Revolution

editorial.author: Klaus Schwab
editorial.description: Klaus Schwab outlines the convergence of digital, physical, and biological technologies he terms the fourth industrial revolution, discussing its potential effects on economies, governance, and society.

canonical_title: 21 Lessons for the 21st Century

editorial.author: Yuval Noah Harari
editorial.description: Yuval Noah Harari presents essays on contemporary issues including technology, AI, work, politics, religion, and education, examining challenges facing societies in the present era.

canonical_title: Solomon's Code: Humanity in a World of Thinking Machines

editorial.author: Olaf Groth, Mark Nitzberg
editorial.description: Olaf Groth and Mark Nitzberg survey developments in artificial intelligence and their ethical and social implications, drawing on expert interviews to discuss governance and human values.

canonical_title: In Our Own Image: Savior or Destroyer? The History and Future of AI

editorial.author: George Zarkadakis
editorial.description: George Zarkadakis traces the history of artificial intelligence alongside myths and ideas about artificial beings, examining the science and cultural narratives behind thinking machines.

canonical_title: Heartificial Intelligence: Embracing Our Humanity to Maximize Machines

editorial.author: John C. Havens
editorial.description: John Havens argues for incorporating human values and ethical measures into the design of AI and automation, discussing wellbeing metrics as an alternative to purely economic ones.

canonical_title: Alone Together: Why We Expect More from Technology and Less from Each Other

editorial.author: Sherry Turkle
editorial.description: Sherry Turkle examines how robots and constant connectivity affect human relationships and intimacy, drawing on interviews and fieldwork on social robots and networked communication.

canonical_title: The Second Self: Computers and the Human Spirit

editorial.author: Sherry Turkle
editorial.description: Sherry Turkle's study of how people relate psychologically to personal computers, based on interviews exploring computers as a presence shaping identity and thought.

canonical_title: The New Breed: What Our History with Animals Reveals about Our Future with Robots

editorial.author: Kate Darling
editorial.description: Kate Darling uses the history of human relationships with animals as an analogy for understanding how people may integrate and relate to robots socially and legally.

canonical_title: Turned On: Science, Sex and Robots

editorial.author: Kate Devlin
editorial.description: Kate Devlin examines the science and culture surrounding sex robots and human intimacy with machines, covering the technology, its history, and social and ethical questions.

canonical_title: Robots and the People Who Love Them

editorial.author: Eve Herold
editorial.description: Eve Herold examines emerging social and companion robots and the relationships people form with them, discussing the technology and its psychological and ethical implications.

canonical_title: Artificial Intimacy: Virtual Friends, Digital Lovers, and Algorithmic Matchmakers

editorial.author: Rob Brooks
editorial.description: Examines how artificial intelligence, robots, and algorithms are reshaping human relationships, sex, and companionship, drawing on evolutionary biology to consider digital partners and matchmaking technologies.

canonical_title: The Artist in the Machine: The World of AI-Powered Creativity

editorial.author: Arthur I. Miller
editorial.description: Surveys artificial intelligence systems that produce visual art, music, and literature, profiling researchers and projects and discussing how machine creativity compares to human creativity.

canonical_title: The Uncanny Muse: Music, Art, and Machines from Automata to AI

editorial.author: David Hajdu
editorial.description: Traces the history of machines in art and music from mechanical automata to modern AI, examining how technology has shaped creative production across centuries.

canonical_title: The Battle for Your Brain: Defending the Right to Think Freely in the Age of Neurotechnology

editorial.author: Nita A. Farahany
editorial.description: Discusses neurotechnology that can read and alter brain activity, arguing for legal recognition of cognitive liberty and mental privacy as the technology advances.

canonical_title: Wired for War: The Robotics Revolution and Conflict in the 21st Century

editorial.author: P. W. Singer
editorial.description: Examines the rise of military robotics and unmanned systems, reporting on their development and use and the ethical, legal, and strategic questions they raise for warfare.

canonical_title: The Kill Chain: Defending America in the Future of High-Tech Warfare

editorial.author: Christian Brose
editorial.description: Argues that future warfare depends on networked sensors and decision speed rather than legacy platforms, and critiques U.S. defense procurement in light of emerging technologies.

canonical_title: Autonomy: The Quest to Build the Driverless Car

editorial.author: Lawrence D. Burns, Christopher Shulgan
editorial.description: Chronicles the development of self-driving car technology, drawing on the author's leadership of an early autonomous vehicle program and the broader industry race.

canonical_title: Driverless: Intelligent Cars and the Road Ahead

editorial.author: Hod Lipson, Melba Kurman
editorial.description: Explains the technology behind autonomous vehicles and discusses their expected effects on transportation, cities, and daily life.

canonical_title: The Man from the Future: The Visionary Life of John von Neumann

editorial.author: Ananyo Bhattacharya
editorial.description: Biography of mathematician John von Neumann, covering his contributions to game theory, computing, quantum mechanics, and the development of nuclear weapons.

canonical_title: Rise of the Machines: A Cybernetic History

editorial.author: Thomas Rid
editorial.description: Traces the history of cybernetics and the concept of the machine from World War II through the digital age, covering its influence on computing, control, and culture.

canonical_title: Mind as Machine: A History of Cognitive Science (2 vols.)

editorial.author: Margaret A. Boden
editorial.description: Two-volume history of cognitive science, tracing the development of ideas about mind across psychology, artificial intelligence, neuroscience, linguistics, and philosophy.

canonical_title: Ada's Algorithm: How Lord Byron's Daughter Ada Lovelace Launched the Digital Age

editorial.author: James Essinger
editorial.description: Biography of Ada Lovelace, daughter of Lord Byron, describing her work with Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine and her notes on early computing.

canonical_title: The Annotated Turing

editorial.author: Charles Petzold
editorial.description: Presents Alan Turing's 1936 paper on computable numbers with line-by-line commentary, explaining the mathematics and the concept of the Turing machine.

canonical_title: Artificial General Intelligence

editorial.author: Ben Goertzel, Cassio Pennachin (eds.)
editorial.description: Edited academic collection of papers on artificial general intelligence, presenting various theoretical approaches and architectures aimed at building human-level machine intelligence.

canonical_title: How to Build a Brain: A Neural Architecture for Biological Cognition

editorial.author: Chris Eliasmith
editorial.description: Describes the author's Semantic Pointer Architecture, a large-scale model of brain function, and presents Spaun, a simulated brain that performs cognitive tasks.

canonical_title: Principles of Synthetic Intelligence: PSI: An Architecture of Motivated Cognition

editorial.author: Joscha Bach
editorial.description: Presents the PSI theory, a cognitive architecture integrating motivation, emotion, and cognition, proposed as a framework for understanding and building intelligent agents.

canonical_title: Failure to Disrupt: Why Technology Alone Can't Transform Education

editorial.author: Justin Reich
editorial.description: Argues that educational technologies have repeatedly failed to transform learning at scale, examining instructor-guided courses, algorithmic tutors, and peer-based platforms.

canonical_title: The Digital Doctor: Hope, Hype, and Harm at the Dawn of Medicine's Computer Age

editorial.author: Robert Wachter
editorial.description: Examines the adoption of electronic health records and computing in medicine, discussing benefits, unintended harms, and the effects of digital systems on healthcare.

canonical_title: 2001: A Space Odyssey

editorial.author: Arthur C. Clarke
editorial.description: Science fiction novel in which a monolith discovered on the Moon leads to a mission to Jupiter, where the ship's intelligent computer HAL 9000 malfunctions and turns against the crew.

canonical_title: The Moon Is a Harsh Mistress

editorial.author: Robert A. Heinlein
editorial.description: Science fiction novel about a lunar penal colony that revolts against Earth's control, aided by a self-aware central computer named Mike.

canonical_title: The Player of Games

editorial.author: Iain M. Banks
editorial.description: Science fiction novel set in the Culture universe, following a master game player recruited to compete in a complex game that underpins a distant empire's social order.

canonical_title: Permutation City

editorial.author: Greg Egan
editorial.description: Science fiction novel exploring copied human consciousnesses run as software, and the launch of a self-evolving artificial universe.

canonical_title: Accelerando

editorial.author: Charles Stross
editorial.description: Science fiction novel tracing three generations of a family across an accelerating technological era marked by artificial intelligence, posthuman minds, and economic transformation.

canonical_title: Daemon

editorial.author: Daniel Suarez
editorial.description: Techno-thriller in which a distributed software program, or daemon, activates after its creator's death and begins reshaping society through networked systems and recruited human agents.

canonical_title: All Systems Red (The Murderbot Diaries #1)

editorial.author: Martha Wells
editorial.description: First novella in the Murderbot Diaries, narrated by a security android that has hacked its own governor module and reluctantly protects a survey team from a deadly threat.

canonical_title: A Closed and Common Orbit

editorial.author: Becky Chambers
editorial.description: Science fiction novel following an artificial intelligence inhabiting a human-like body as it builds a new identity, alongside a parallel story of a cloned girl.

canonical_title: Machinehood

editorial.author: S. B. Divya
editorial.description: Science fiction novel set in 2095 where humans rely on performance-enhancing pills to compete with AI in a gig economy, following a bodyguard who confronts a group called the Machinehood that demands an end to pill production.

canonical_title: The Mountain in the Sea

editorial.author: Ray Nayler
editorial.description: Science fiction novel about a research team studying a possibly intelligent octopus species, raising questions about non-human minds, alongside subplots involving artificial intelligence and automated fishing.

canonical_title: Burn-In: A Novel of the Real Robotic Revolution

editorial.author: P. W. Singer, August Cole
editorial.description: Techno-thriller in which an FBI agent field-tests an advanced humanoid robot while investigating a conspiracy that exploits automation-driven unemployment and unrest in a near-future United States.

canonical_title: QualityLand

editorial.author: Marc-Uwe Kling
editorial.description: Satirical science fiction novel set in an automated, algorithm-driven society, following a man who tries to return a defective product the system insists is correct.

canonical_title: Power and Progress: Our Thousand-Year Struggle Over Technology and Prosperity

editorial.author: Daron Acemoglu, Simon Johnson
editorial.description: Examines the history of technological change and argues that productivity gains from new technologies, including AI, have not automatically benefited workers and require political choices to share broadly.

canonical_title: Android Dreams: The Past, Present and Future of Artificial Intelligence

editorial.author: Toby Walsh
editorial.description: Overview of artificial intelligence covering its history, current capabilities, and possible future developments, written for a general audience.

canonical_title: An Artificial Revolution: On Power, Politics and AI

editorial.author: Ivana Bartoletti
editorial.description: Examines the social and political implications of artificial intelligence, including data, surveillance, gender, and power, and argues for stronger democratic governance.

canonical_title: Working with AI: Real Stories of Human-Machine Collaboration

editorial.author: Thomas H. Davenport, Steven M. Miller
editorial.description: Presents case studies of workers across industries describing how they use AI tools in their jobs, drawn from interviews about human-machine collaboration.

canonical_title: Complexity: A Guided Tour

editorial.author: Melanie Mitchell
editorial.description: Introduction to complex systems science, covering topics such as chaos, computation, evolution, networks, and self-organization for a general readership.

canonical_title: Artificial Life: A Report from the Frontier Where Computers Meet Biology

editorial.author: Steven Levy
editorial.description: Journalistic account of the artificial life research field, describing scientists who use computers to simulate and study lifelike behavior and evolution.

canonical_title: Fluid Concepts and Creative Analogies

editorial.author: Douglas R. Hofstadter, FARG
editorial.description: Collected reports from Hofstadter's Fluid Analogies Research Group describing computer models such as Copycat and Tabletop that simulate analogy-making and the fluidity of concepts in human cognition.

canonical_title: The Pattern on the Stone: The Simple Ideas That Make Computers Work

editorial.author: W. Daniel Hillis
editorial.description: A general-audience explanation of how computers work, covering Boolean logic, circuits, algorithms, programming languages, encryption, and topics including parallel computing and machine learning.

canonical_title: Shadows of the Mind: A Search for the Missing Science of Consciousness

editorial.author: Roger Penrose
editorial.description: Penrose argues that human consciousness and mathematical understanding are non-computational, drawing on Goedel's incompleteness theorem and proposing that quantum processes in the brain underlie consciousness.

canonical_title: The Essential Turing

editorial.author: B. Jack Copeland (ed.)
editorial.description: An edited collection of Alan Turing's writings, including papers on computability, the Turing machine, artificial intelligence, the Turing test, codebreaking, and morphogenesis, with commentary.

canonical_title: Adaptation in Natural and Artificial Systems

editorial.author: John H. Holland
editorial.description: Holland's foundational work introducing genetic algorithms, presenting a mathematical framework for adaptation in biological and artificial systems based on selection and reproductive plans.

canonical_title: Unified Theories of Cognition

editorial.author: Allen Newell
editorial.description: Newell presents the Soar cognitive architecture and argues for unified theories that account for the full range of human cognition within a single computational framework.

canonical_title: Human Problem Solving

editorial.author: Allen Newell, Herbert A. Simon
editorial.description: Newell and Simon's study of human problem solving, presenting information-processing theory, protocol analysis, and the concept of problem spaces and heuristic search.

canonical_title: Scripts, Plans, Goals, and Understanding

editorial.author: Roger C. Schank, Robert P. Abelson
editorial.description: Schank and Abelson develop theories of how knowledge structures such as scripts, plans, and goals are used to represent and understand everyday situations and natural language.

canonical_title: Parallel Distributed Processing: Explorations in the Microstructure of Cognition (Vols. 1-2)

editorial.author: David E. Rumelhart, James L. McClelland, PDP Research Group
editorial.description: A two-volume collection presenting the connectionist approach to cognition, covering neural network models, learning algorithms including backpropagation, and applications to perception and language.

canonical_title: The Cambridge Handbook of Artificial Intelligence

editorial.author: Keith Frankish, William M. Ramsey (eds.)
editorial.description: An edited handbook of survey chapters covering the philosophy, history, methods, and subfields of artificial intelligence, including reasoning, learning, robotics, and ethics.

canonical_title: AI: Its Nature and Future

editorial.author: Margaret A. Boden
editorial.description: A short introduction by Boden surveying what artificial intelligence is, its main approaches and history, and questions about its future and relationship to human minds.

canonical_title: Technopoly: The Surrender of Culture to Technology

editorial.author: Neil Postman
editorial.description: Postman's 1992 argument that modern societies have moved from using tools to being governed by them, reaching a state he calls technopoly: the deification of technology, in which culture seeks its authorization, satisfaction, and orders from technology itself. He traces how a surplus of information, generated by technology and then managed by more technology, erodes the moral and institutional frameworks that once gave information meaning. Predating the web, it reads as a foundational text for later critical-technology scholarship and is frequently cited as an ancestor of the contemporary critical-AI literature.

canonical_title: Islands in the Cyberstream: Seeking Havens of Reason in a Programmed Society

editorial.author: Joseph Weizenbaum
editorial.description: A late, dialogic work by the creator of ELIZA, drawn from conversations with Gunna Wendt, extending the humanist skepticism of his 1976 Computer Power and Human Reason. Weizenbaum argues for preserving spaces of human reason and judgment against the encroachment of computational logic into domains where it does not belong. It functions as a coda to one of the earliest and most influential insider critiques of artificial intelligence.

canonical_title: Data Grab: The New Colonialism of Big Tech and How to Fight Back

editorial.author: Ulises A. Mejias and Nick Couldry
editorial.description: Mejias and Couldry frame the mass appropriation of human data as a continuation of historical colonialism, arguing that the extraction of life into data parallels the seizure of land and labour in earlier centuries. Building on their earlier concept of data colonialism, the book maps the ideological and material mechanisms of contemporary data capture and surveys emerging forms of resistance. It sits in the political-economy wing of the critical-AI canon.

canonical_title: Teaching Machines: The History of Personalized Learning

editorial.author: Audrey Watters
editorial.description: Watters traces the century-long history of attempts to mechanize and personalize instruction, from Sidney Pressey and B.F. Skinner's teaching machines to the ed-tech and adaptive-learning systems that anticipate today's AI tutors. The book shows that the dream of automating education long predates computing, and that its recurring promises and failures illuminate present claims about AI in the classroom. A historical corrective to ed-tech solutionism.

canonical_title: The Mechanic and the Luddite: A Ruthless Criticism of Technology and Capitalism

editorial.author: Jathan Sadowski
editorial.description: Sadowski argues that technology under capitalism is engineered to serve accumulation rather than human need, and reclaims the Luddites as clear-eyed critics rather than reflexive opponents of progress. The book uses the figures of the mechanic and the Luddite to think about how technologies could be built and governed differently. Part of the recent wave of explicitly anti-capitalist technology criticism.

canonical_title: Breaking Things at Work: The Luddites Are Right About Why You Hate Your Job

editorial.author: Gavin Mueller
editorial.description: Mueller offers a Marxist reinterpretation of Luddism, arguing that resistance to workplace technology has been a rational response to deskilling, surveillance, and the intensification of labour rather than mere technophobia. The book connects historical machine-breaking to contemporary struggles over automation and platform work. A concise statement of the decelerationist position in labour politics.

canonical_title: Disabling Intelligences: Legacies of Eugenics and How We are Wrong about AI

editorial.author: Rua M. Williams
editorial.description: Williams examines connections between the history of eugenics and the AI industry, arguing through critical disability studies that eugenic assumptions shape how AI is built and how it affects disabled people.

canonical_title: Artifictional Intelligence: Against Humanity's Surrender to Computers

editorial.author: Harry Collins
editorial.description: The sociologist of science Harry Collins argues that the deep difficulty of encoding tacit, socially acquired human knowledge places hard limits on artificial intelligence, and warns that the greater danger is humans lowering their own standards to meet machines rather than machines genuinely matching humans. Drawing on his long work on expertise and the sociology of knowledge, he offers a Turing-test-centred critique of overclaiming. A philosophically grounded skeptical position.

canonical_title: Human Power: Seven Traits for the Politics of the AI Machine Age

editorial.author: Gry Hasselbalch
editorial.description: Hasselbalch examines seven human traits (creativity, intuition, emotion, life, defiance, love, and wisdom) and argues for keeping human power central in the politics of AI and technology.

canonical_title: Imagination: A Manifesto

editorial.author: Ruha Benjamin
editorial.description: Benjamin argues that imagination is a contested political resource, and that the narrow imaginations of powerful technologists have foreclosed more just possible futures. A short manifesto extending the race-and-technology analysis of her earlier work toward a constructive call for collective, liberatory world-building. Positioned against techno-determinist accounts of the future.

canonical_title: Another Science is Possible: A Manifesto for Slow Science

editorial.author: Isabelle Stengers
editorial.description: The philosopher of science Isabelle Stengers critiques the acceleration and marketization of research, arguing that fast science driven by competition and commercial pressure undermines careful, publicly accountable knowledge. The book makes the case for slow science as a collective practice. Relevant to critical-AI debates about research culture, benchmarks, and the pace of deployment.

canonical_title: Artificial Whiteness: Politics and Ideology in Artificial Intelligence

editorial.author: Yarden Katz
editorial.description: Katz argues that AI functions as an ideology bound up with whiteness and colonial power, serving as a flexible instrument for reasserting existing hierarchies under the appearance of objectivity. Drawing on critical race theory and the history of the field, the book reads AI's recurring booms as political rather than purely technical phenomena. A theoretically dense entry in the critical canon.

canonical_title: Technocolonialism: When Technology for Good is Harmful

editorial.author: Mirca Madianou
editorial.description: Madianou examines how humanitarian and development uses of data and AI can reproduce colonial relations, arguing that technology-for-good initiatives often entrench inequality and extraction even as they claim to alleviate it. Grounded in empirical study of humanitarian operations. Extends the data-colonialism literature into the aid and crisis context.

canonical_title: The Shame Machine: Who Profits in the New Age of Humiliation

editorial.author: Cathy O'Neil
editorial.description: O'Neil, author of Weapons of Math Destruction, examines how shame is manufactured and monetized, including by algorithmic systems and platforms that profit from humiliation and outrage. The book distinguishes punching-down shaming from legitimate accountability. A more sociological companion to her earlier work on algorithmic harm.

canonical_title: Datafied Childhoods: Data Practices and Imaginaries in Children's Lives

editorial.author: Giovanna Mascheroni and Andra Siibak
editorial.description: Mascheroni and Siibak examine how datafication, algorithms, and AI shape children's lives at home, school, and in relationships, drawing on research into the data practices of children, parents, and teachers.

canonical_title: Why Machines Will Never Rule the World: Artificial Intelligence without Fear

editorial.author: Jobst Landgrebe and Barry Smith
editorial.description: Landgrebe and Smith mount a mathematical and philosophical argument that artificial general intelligence is impossible in principle, contending that the complex systems underlying human cognition cannot be captured by computable models. The ontologist Barry Smith brings a formal-philosophy lens to the limits-of-AI debate. One of the strongest stated impossibility arguments in the skeptical literature.

canonical_title: The Unaccountability Machine: Why Big Systems Make Terrible Decisions

editorial.author: Dan Davies
editorial.description: Davies uses management cybernetics and the idea of accountability sinks, structures that absorb responsibility so that no individual can be blamed, to explain how large organizations and automated systems produce disastrous decisions no one owns. Drawing on Stafford Beer's systems thinking, it speaks directly to questions of algorithmic accountability. Of particular relevance to AI governance and the diffusion-of-responsibility problem.

canonical_title: Technology Is Not Neutral: A Short Guide to Technology Ethics

editorial.author: Stephanie Hare
editorial.description: Hare offers an accessible practitioner's guide to technology ethics, arguing against the common claim that tools are neutral and surveying real cases across surveillance, biometrics, and AI. The book is aimed at decision-makers who need a working ethical vocabulary. A bridge between academic critique and applied governance.

canonical_title: The Ethics of Privacy and Surveillance

editorial.author: Carissa Veliz
editorial.description: A scholarly treatment by the author of Privacy Is Power, developing the philosophical foundations of why privacy matters and how surveillance harms individuals and societies. More academic than her trade book, it offers a sustained normative argument. Relevant to data-protection and surveillance debates in AI.

canonical_title: Ethics for People Who Work in Tech

editorial.author: Marc Steen
editorial.description: Steen provides a practical ethics handbook for technology practitioners, drawing on virtue ethics and design methods to help teams reason about the systems they build. Oriented toward everyday professional decisions rather than high theory. A applied-ethics entry useful for practitioner training.

canonical_title: Distrust: Big Data, Data-Torturing, and the Assault on Science

editorial.author: Gary Smith
editorial.description: Smith examines how the misuse of big data, p-hacking, and spurious correlation undermines trust in science, and warns that data-driven and AI methods amplify these failures when divorced from theory. The book is a statistician's caution against pattern-finding without reasoning. Relevant to debates over evidence quality in ML-based research.

canonical_title: Oxford Handbook of Digital Ethics

editorial.author: Carissa Veliz (ed.)
editorial.description: An edited scholarly reference collecting authoritative essays across digital ethics, including AI, privacy, algorithmic fairness, and online harms. Functions as a survey of the academic field rather than a single argument. A reference-grade volume for the governance shelf.

canonical_title: Superbloom: How Technologies of Connection Tear Us Apart

editorial.author: Nicholas Carr
editorial.description: Carr, author of The Shallows, argues that communication technologies promising connection have instead produced fragmentation, misunderstanding, and emotional strain. The book extends his long-running critique of how media reshape cognition and society into the era of algorithmic feeds and AI mediation. A measured, widely-reviewed entry.

canonical_title: The Internet Con: How to Seize the Means of Computation

editorial.author: Cory Doctorow
editorial.description: Doctorow makes an interoperability-centred argument for dismantling Big Tech's lock-in, proposing that mandated interoperability would let users escape walled gardens and restore competition. A concise policy polemic from a prominent digital-rights advocate. Frames the structural-power dimension of platform and AI concentration.

canonical_title: Enshittification: Why Everything Suddenly Got Worse and What to Do About It

editorial.author: Cory Doctorow
editorial.description: Doctorow develops his widely-cited concept of enshittification, the process by which platforms degrade for users and business customers alike as value is extracted for shareholders, into a book-length account with proposed remedies. The coined term entered mainstream discourse before the book. A signature statement of contemporary platform critique.

canonical_title: Scorched Earth: Beyond the Digital Age to a Post-Capitalist World

editorial.author: Jonathan Crary
editorial.description: Crary argues that the internet complex is incompatible with an ecologically survivable future and calls for its dismantling as part of a post-capitalist transformation. An uncompromising anti-digital polemic from an art and culture theorist. Represents the radical-refusal pole of technology criticism.

canonical_title: Slow Computing: Why We Need Balanced Digital Lives

editorial.author: Rob Kitchin and Alistair Fraser
editorial.description: Kitchin and Fraser propose slow computing as an individual and collective response to data extraction and digital overload, offering both critique and practical strategies for reclaiming time and autonomy. Grounded in critical data studies. Pairs analysis with a constructive programme.

canonical_title: The Digital Innovation Race: Conceptualizing the Emerging New World Order

editorial.author: Cecilia Rikap and Bengt-Ake Lundvall
editorial.description: Rikap and Lundvall analyze how a handful of intellectual-monopoly corporations capture innovation and shape the geopolitics of digital technology, including AI. The book combines innovation economics with political economy to explain corporate and national power. Relevant to compute, IP, and sovereignty debates.

canonical_title: The Economy of Algorithms: AI and the Rise of the Digital Minions

editorial.author: Marek Kowalkiewicz
editorial.description: Kowalkiewicz describes an emerging economy in which vast numbers of small autonomous algorithms act on our behalf, reshaping business and daily life. The book is more analytical than polemical, mapping the agentic-software shift. A business-facing complement to the critical literature.

canonical_title: Rhythm and Vigilance: Ethnographies of Surveillance and Time

editorial.author: Vita Peacock et al.
editorial.description: An edited collection of ethnographic studies examining how surveillance and monitoring technologies affect the experience of time, drawing on fieldwork from Europe, China, and the United States.

canonical_title: The Tensions of Algorithmic Thinking: Automation, Intelligence and the Politics of Knowing

editorial.author: David Beer
editorial.description: Beer examines how algorithmic thinking reshapes the politics of knowledge, exploring the tensions between automation and human judgment in how we come to know things. A sociologist of data continues his analysis of metrics and algorithmic culture. Theoretical entry on epistemology and automation.

canonical_title: Postgrowth Digital Futures: Digital Technologies, Degrowth, and Radical Abundance

editorial.author: Sy Taffel
editorial.description: Taffel examines digital technologies through the lens of degrowth and postgrowth thought, proposing principles such as conviviality, limits, decommodification, and radical abundance for sustainable digital futures.

canonical_title: La Silicolonizacion del Mundo

editorial.author: Eric Sadin
editorial.description: Sadin traces the trajectory of Silicon Valley and argues that connected objects and AI extend a techno-liberal model that organizes society algorithmically and erodes individual and collective decision-making.

canonical_title: La Inteligencia Artificial o el Desafio del Siglo

editorial.author: Eric Sadin
editorial.description: Sadin examines artificial intelligence through the history of ideas and a phenomenology of technology, arguing that AI represents a form of radical antihumanism with epistemological, ontological, and political consequences.

canonical_title: La Era del Individuo Tirano

editorial.author: Eric Sadin
editorial.description: A philosophical analysis tracing the rise of what Sadin calls the tyrannical individual, linking liberal individualism, neoliberal culture, and digital platforms to phenomena such as polarization, conspiracy theories, and the erosion of a shared common world.

canonical_title: More than Words: How to Think About Writing in the Age of AI

editorial.author: John Warner
editorial.description: Warner argues that generative AI should prompt a rethinking of why and how we teach writing, contending that writing is thinking and cannot be outsourced without loss. The book defends human writing as a cognitive and humane practice while engaging seriously with the tools. A measured educator's response to LLMs in the classroom.

canonical_title: Data Conscience: Algorithmic Siege on our Humanity

editorial.author: Brandeis Marshall
editorial.description: An examination of how data systems and algorithms encode and reinforce racial and social inequities, arguing that data practices are not neutral and proposing approaches for more accountable, equitable computing.

canonical_title: Medium Hot: Images in the Age of Heat

editorial.author: Hito Steyerl
editorial.description: An essay collection using thermodynamic metaphors of heat and entropy to examine AI, large language models, and algorithmic image-making, addressing their energy use, labor exploitation, and military applications, drawn partly from the author's own art experiments.

canonical_title: The Nerd Reich: Silicon Valley Fascism and the War on Democracy

editorial.author: Gil Duran
editorial.description: A journalistic account of a tech-authoritarian movement, tracing ideological roots through figures such as Peter Thiel, Marc Andreessen, and Elon Musk, and arguing they promote replacing democratic government with corporate and technological rule.
